_note: 2026-07-14 batch: 14 yeshivot/seminaries surfaced by a targeted research round covering the DB's actual coverage gap (Hebrew-medium Chareidi/Sephardic/Chassidish, plus remaining unmined Anglo seminary/yeshiva directories), presented to the user as an Artifact for review before import. User approved 14 of 27 researched candidates ('the solid entries') for this batch; the rest were held back deliberately: 'Yeshivat Heichal Yerushalayim' was dropped as a CONFIRMED DUPLICATE of the already-published heichal-yerushalayim (identical website/email verified by direct DB lookup before writing this file); 'The Breslov Yeshiva' (Har Nof) was excluded outright on safety grounds (its listed leader has a syndicated content connection to Rabbi Eliezer Berland, a convicted sex offender whose following is widely considered cult-like); 'Yeshivat Porat Yosef' and 'Yeshivat Or HaChaim' were held pending a program-fit judgment call (no confirmed post-high-school entry track / explicitly sends rather than receives overseas students); 9 Hebrew-only Litvish/Sephardic yeshivot (Slabodka, Orchos Torah, Beer Yaakov, Knesses Chizkiyahu, Tifrach, Kaminetz, Ohr Yisrael, Netivot Yaakov, Hazon Ovadia) were left out of any import file because none has a working website or confirmed overseas-admissions path -- an unlistable entry for this directory, not a research gap. Affiliation/hashkafa was independently verified beyond each program's own site where noted in research; several are 'self-described only' and deliberately carry no affiliation tag below rather than guess one. contactEmail values below were observed directly on each program's official page (never guessed) and are intentionally included per the import pipeline's convention -- import-researched.ts routes them to data/ignored-import-emails-<date>.json for the separate contact-verification workflow rather than writing them onto Program directly. No pricing/tuition/session-date data included anywhere. All 14 tag slugs used below were confirmed to already exist in the DB before writing this file (read-only pre-flight check) to avoid minting duplicate tags.

anglo_seminaries.name: Baer Miriam (Ma'ayanot)
anglo_seminaries.description: A one-year seminary program in Har Nof, Jerusalem, for young women pursuing Torah study through a textual, contextual, and hashkafic approach.
anglo_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the seminary's website.

anglo_seminaries.name: Sharfman's (Bnot Torah Institute)
anglo_seminaries.description: A women's seminary in Ramat Eshkol, Jerusalem, offering a gap-year program of Torah study with a curriculum incorporating Sephardic learning traditions.
anglo_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the seminary's website.

anglo_seminaries.name: Machon Maayan
anglo_seminaries.description: A one-year Modern Orthodox women's seminary in Beit Shemesh, run by the Orthodox Union (OU) Israel Center.
anglo_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the seminary's website.

anglo_seminaries.name: MMY (Michlelet Mevaseret Yerushalayim)
anglo_seminaries.description: A Religious Zionist women's seminary in Jerusalem offering an 8-10 month gap-year program of Torah study.
anglo_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the seminary's website.

anglo_seminaries.name: Sha'alvim for Women (SFW)
anglo_seminaries.description: The women's division of Yeshivat Sha'alvim, offering a Religious Zionist gap-year seminary program in Jerusalem.
anglo_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the seminary's website.

anglo_seminaries.name: She'arim (College of Jewish Studies for Women)
anglo_seminaries.description: A women's college of Jewish studies in Har Nof, Jerusalem, combining Torah study with a Hebrew ulpan component.
anglo_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the seminary's website.

anglo_seminaries.name: Tiferet Center
anglo_seminaries.description: A Religious Zionist women's seminary in Ramat Beit Shemesh offering a one-year 'Shana Alef' program, primarily for North American students.
anglo_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the seminary's website.

anglo_yeshivot.name: Imrei Binah
anglo_yeshivot.description: A men's yeshiva in Har Nof, Jerusalem, for post-high-school Torah study.
anglo_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the yeshiva's website.

anglo_yeshivot.name: Yeshivas Aderes Hatorah (Rav Senter's)
anglo_yeshivot.description: A men's yeshiva in Jerusalem founded by Rabbi Chaim Zvi Senter for post-high-school Torah study.
anglo_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the yeshiva's website.

anglo_yeshivot.name: Bais Medrash L'Torah (Portnoy's Yeshiva)
anglo_yeshivot.description: A men's yeshiva in Jerusalem founded by Rabbi Shaye Portnoy for post-high-school Torah study.
anglo_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the yeshiva's website.

anglo_yeshivot.name: Nishmat (Liba Shana Ba'Aretz)
anglo_yeshivot.description: A gap-year program at Nishmat in Jerusalem combining English-speaking staff and classes with an Israeli, Hebrew-language beit midrash.
anglo_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the program's website.

sephardic_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Yesod HaTorah
sephardic_yeshivot.description: A Sephardic-oriented men's yeshiva in Ramat Shlomo, Jerusalem, offering a one-year program of Torah study accredited for transcript purposes by several North American colleges.
sephardic_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the yeshiva's website.

sephardic_yeshivot.name: Kisse Rahamim Yeshivah
sephardic_yeshivot.description: A Sephardic yeshiva in Bnei Brak preserving Tunisian Jewish learning traditions, spanning a full track from mesivta through kollel.
sephardic_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the yeshiva's website.

chassidish_yeshivot.name: Yeshivas Ne'imus HaTorah
chassidish_yeshivot.description: An English-speaking Breslov yeshiva in the Old City of Tzfat, offering Torah study centered on the teachings of Rebbe Nachman of Breslov.
chassidish_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the yeshiva's website.
